title: -Contract No. 32100079 Amendment: ePlus Technology Inc., for Cohesity Backup Software, Maintenance and Support

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32100079 to
increase the spending authority with ePlus Technology Inc. (“ePlus”), for Cohesity backup
software, maintenance and support services increasing the amount by $110,000 for a
total not-to-exceed amount of $718,400 from November 1, 2020 to November 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for software maintenance and support services in the amount of $110,000 is
allocated for Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 in the Department of Information Technology’s Cost
Allocation Fund. Spending for software, maintenance and support in future fiscal years is
subject to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriation
ordinances.
sections.current_situation:
The City currently utilizes Cohesity DataProtect (“DP”), provided through ePlus, as its
enterprise backup and disaster recovery (“DR”) solution for email, websites, applications,
and database servers. DP is a modern, cloud-based platform that streamlines and
simplifies backup and recovery processes, delivering efficiency gains through faster
backups, quicker and more reliable recovery, and enhanced security. The system also
reduces administrative burden by requiring less staff intervention, while its advanced
capabilities ensure resilience and adaptability to the City’s evolving technological needs.
Staff have found DP to be consistently reliable, providing effective and dependable
backup and recovery capabilities. Based on this performance, staff recommend extending
maintenance and support through November 2026 to ensure the City’s backup and
disaster recovery systems continue to operate effectively, supporting daily operations and
the delivery of essential services to the Berkeley community.
The DP and DR solution, provided by ePlus, supports the City’s Strategic Plan goal to
create a resilient, safe, connected, and prepared City.

Contract Amendment: ePlus Technology Inc. for
Cohesity Software, Maintenance and Support CONSENT CALENDAR
November 18, 2025
sections.background:
In February 2020, the City issued Request for Proposals No. 20-11386-C for a new
enterprise backup and disaster recovery solution. The City received four qualifying
responses, which were evaluated by a review committee against operational,
technological, and fiscal requirements. Following this competitive evaluation process, the
committee determined that the proposal from ePlus for the DP solution with hosted Cloud
Storage best met the City’s needs. This implementation has provided the City with five
years of reliable backup and recovery capabilities.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The DP solution eliminates the City’s reliance on approximately 350 magnetic tape
cartridges each year. The manufacturing and recycling of these cartridges did not align
with the City’s Green IT goals, and their elimination reduces environmental impact.
In addition, the solution removes the need to transport backup tapes to and from offsite
storage facilities. This change not only reduces transportation costs but also decreases
greenhouse gas emissions, further supporting the City’s sustainability objectives while
improving operational efficiency.
sections.rationale:
Renewing maintenance and support through November 2026 ensures the City continues
to benefit from vendor updates, technical support, and security enhancements, while
maintaining the stability of a proven platform that plays a critical role in daily operations
and service delivery to the Berkeley community.
sections.alternatives:
At this time, staff does not recommend pursuing an alternative platform, as the current
solution continues to meet the City’s operational needs effectively. Staff recommend a
one-year renewal to ensure continuity of service while providing an opportunity to re-
assess competitive market offerings that address the City’s long-term backup and
disaster recovery requirements via a new RFP.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Department of Information Technology, 981-6541

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###–N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32100079 AMENDMENT: EPLUS TECHNOLOGY INC. FOR
COHESITY BACKUP SOFTWARE, MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley requires a reliable, secure, and efficient enterprise
backup and disaster recovery system to safeguard critical production email, websites,
applications, and database servers; and
WHEREAS, in February 2020 the City issued Request for Proposals No. 20-11386-C to
replace its aging and inefficient tape-based backup system, which no longer met
operational requirements; and
WHEREAS, after a competitive evaluation process, the City selected ePlus Technology
Inc. (“ePlus”) to provide implementation services for the Cohesity DataProtect solution
(“DP”); and
WHEREAS, on July 28, 2020, by resolution No. 69,520-N.S., the City Council authorized
the City Manager to execute a contract with ePlus for DP; and
WHEREAS, continued vendor maintenance and support are necessary to ensure ongoing
access to software updates, security patches, and technical assistance, thereby
maintaining the resiliency of critical City systems; and
WHEREAS, funding for software, maintenance, and support is available in the
Department of Information Technology’s Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 Cost Allocation Fund, and
spending in future fiscal years is subject to Council approval of the proposed citywide
budget and annual appropriation ordinances.
NOW THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the City
Manager or delegate is authorized to amend Contract No. 32100079 to increase the
spending authority with ePlus, for DP software, maintenance and support services
increasing the amount by $110,000 for a total not-to-exceed amount of $718,400 from
November 1, 2020 to November 30, 2026.
